exemplar.title: Las Bicicletas Son Para El Verano

detailedExemplarData.title: Las Bicicletas Son Para El Verano

subject.title: Spanish B
subject.description:
**IB Spanish B** is a **Group 2: Language Acquisition** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **five prescribed themes: Identities, Experiences, Human ingenuity, Social organisation, and Sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive (listening and reading), productive (writing), and interactive (speaking) language skills while fostering intercultural understanding through exploration of Spanish-speaking cultures**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2020**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Writing task (choose 1 of 3 options) | 1h 15m | 1h 30m | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Reading and listening comprehension | 1h 45m | 2h | 50% |
| **Individual Oral (IA)** | Oral presentation and discussion | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 25% |

strengths: Wide and varied vocabulary with occasional idiomatic turns enhancing nuance
Effective use of a range of grammatical structures (tenses, clauses, modals)
High degree of accuracyâerrors are minor and do not hinder communication
Pronunciation and intonation (inferred through spelling approximations) are clear and support intelligibility
weaknesses: Frequent orthographic and accentuation errors (e.g., lentajes/lentejas, perdida/pÃ©rdida)
Occasional anglicisms (service, future) and mis-selected words reduce precision
Some overly long or complex sentences lack proper punctuation
Agreement and mood-choice errors appear sporadically
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are recurrent orthographic slips and a handful of mis-selected terms, the candidate consistently demonstrates nuanced vocabulary, a wide variety of grammatical structures and a high level of accuracy. Messages remain clear throughout, and the inferred pronunciation profile is very strong. Taken together, these attributes fully satisfy the descriptors for the top band (10â12), warranting the maximum score.

strengths: All commentaries remain tightly focused on the extract and convincingly link scenes to wider themes
Consistent and effective use of textual details (symbols, contrasts, plot locations) to support interpretations
Demonstrates insight (e.g., irony of pan/bombardeos, loss of innocence) at every turn
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally lapses into descriptive summary rather than deeper critical evaluation
Some redundancy in introductory phrasing across commentaries
Occasional reliance on general statements without line-by-line quotation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs presentation is unfailingly relevant and always substantiated with well-developed observations. Commentary moves beyond mere paraphrase to offer convincing thematic and symbolic readings, meeting the highest descriptor for consistent relevance and sophisticated development. Therefore, the maximum six marks are justified.

strengths: Responses are uniformly relevant and show logical development
Answers anticipate likely questions with clear, on-topic content and textual links
Personal engagement and interpretive insights suggest strong conversational potential
weaknesses: Lack of live interaction means spontaneity cannot be directly verified
Depth of elaboration sometimes remains at a moral or emotional level rather than fully analytical
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although evidence is indirect, the written responses demonstrate consistent relevance, appropriate register and clear development. The candidateâs ability to link ideas and offer personal interpretations aligns with the highest band for response quality and depth, meriting the full six marks.

strengths: Sustained comprehension is evident through coherent, context-rich replies
Participation (inferred from notes) shows initiative and independent contributions
Links between questions and textual examples are consistently strong
weaknesses: Some lexical gaps could prompt occasional clarification
Absence of a live recording means turn-taking and real-time repair strategies are not directly observable
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidateâs prepared material and responses indicate consistently sustained comprehension and active participation. Independent contributions and clear evidence of interaction quality meet the descriptors for the top band. As such, six marks are awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: Across the eight mini-commentaries the student handles present, past and conditional tenses, causal clauses, relative clauses and modal constructions, showing some ambition (â¦podrÃ­a interpretarse, â¦se habrÃ¡n dado cuenta).  Yet agreement, mood choice and spelling frequently falter (estÃ¡ muy lejos, occuriera, se estan desapareciendo), and complex syntax is often avoided through coordination.  Variety is evident but not consistently effective, placing the work in the 4-6 band; given the frequency of flawed complex attempts, a lower-mid value of 4 is appropriate.

Although no recording is provided, the volume of phonetic approximations in spelling (lentajas/lentejas, secaulas/secuelas) suggests a generally intelligible but imperfect pronunciation profile typical of HL learners.  In absence of evidence of serious interference, a mid-band judgement (5) is the fairest conservative estimate.

The breadth of commentary indicates the student could take initiative (comparing scenes, drawing thematic parallels).  Yet because interaction evidence is indirect, assigning the very top level would be speculative.  A 3 recognises probable sustained participation without overstating undocumented independence.

Every commentary stays tightly anchored to its designated extract, summarising the segment, locating it in the plot and drawing thematic links (bicicleta sÃ­mbolo, bombardeos, solidaridad).  The focus never drifts to unrelated personal anecdotes.  The explanation is convincing if occasionally repetitive, fully meeting the âconsistently relevantâ descriptor; a score of 5 reflects this strong alignment.

The reflections display some analytical depth (relaciÃ³n epÃ­logo-prÃ³logo, ironÃ­a del pan), but elaboration often stops at surface moral reactions (âme hace muy tristeâ).  Personal interpretation is present yet not extensively developed.  This situates the work solidly in the middle band, and a 3 is appropriate.

While no live interview is shown, the written responses indicate the student would answer questions with clear, on-topic content, linking ideas across the play.  The breadth of prepared material implies an ability to provide consistently relevant answers.  This merits the top band (5-6) in principle; however, as spontaneity cannot be verified, a cautious 4 within the 3-4 competence range is awarded.

Prepared notes point to a capacity to follow questions, reference specific scenes and link ideas, suggesting mostly sustained comprehension.  Gaps in lexical accuracy could occasionally require clarification, so full consistency (5-6) is not secure.  Hence, upper-middle band value 3 is selected.

The student quotes line numbers, paraphrases dialogue and offers interpretation (la bicicleta como libertad, el contraste pan/bombas).  Support is usually present but occasionally remains general (âesto demuestra el impacto de la guerraâ without textual proof).  Depth is competent rather than penetrating, fitting the 3-4 band; the higher value (4) recognises the range of scenes covered.

The student employs a reasonably wide lexical range (pÃ©rdida de la inocencia, solidaridad entre vecinos, sÃ­mbolo clave, contraste irÃ³nico), moves comfortably between registers (narra, comenta, evalÃºa) and occasionally risks idiomatic turns (a mi modo de ver, cabe destacar).  However, there is no sustained, sophisticated idiom and several mis-selected words or anglicisms (service, future, crew) limit nuance.  Overall, vocabulary is generally appropriate and varied, fitting securely in the 4-6 band; a mid-band score of 5 is justified.

Despite numerous spelling and accent errors (lentajes, relazioncar, differencia), ideas remain clear and messages rarely require re-reading.  Basic structures are generally accurate; errors in more complex segments (hubiera *occuriera*, *le va ha dar*) occasionally obscure nuance but not overall comprehension.  This aligns with âmostly accurate â¦ errors sometimes interfereâ.  A central score of 5 best reflects that balance.

annotations.comment: Typo âconstantementâ should read âconstantementeâ. Such slips accumulate and reduce perceived proficiency.

Frequent orthographic errors such as âpersonaiesâ instead of âpersonajesâ and missing accents (âperdidaâ â âpÃ©rdidaâ) hinder clarity. Recommend systematic proofreading or using spell-check.
Opening with âY por esoâ is repetitive and overly colloquial. Vary connectors (por consiguiente, por lo tanto) to achieve a more formal academic tone.
This summary of scene context is clear but remains descriptive. To strengthen analysis, integrate quotations and develop specific thematic interpretations (e.g., innocence vs. harsh reality).
Missing accent in âopinionâ (â âopiniÃ³nâ) and inconsistent register (âCreo que todos estamosâ). Maintain formal register and accurate accentuation.
The recurring introductory sentence on page 2 is redundant. Consider consolidating background information to focus each commentary on distinct analytical points.
Spelling error: âpossibleâ should read âposibleâ. Also âuna de las temasâ â âuno de los temasâ. Review gender-number agreement and spelling.
Anglicism âserviceâ and misspelling âmiliarâ (â âmilitarâ) disrupt precision. Favor Spanish equivalents and check technical vocabulary.
Typographical errors âconstandeâ and insertion of English âfutureâ weaken fluency. Replace with âconstanteâ and âfuturoâ to maintain cohesion.
Missing accent in âpudiÃ³â (should be âpudoâ) and âestanâ â âestÃ¡nâ. Accurate accentuation is essential for clear communication.
Spelling âcommentariosâ should be âcomentariosâ and missing accent âÃ©lâ. Proofreading will help eliminate such slips.
Analysis of the bicycle symbol shows potential but remains surface-level. Develop interpretation by linking specific dialogue or stage directions to thematic impact.
Repeating âA mi modo de verâ each commentary reduces variety. Vary phrasing (por mi parte, considero que) to improve stylistic nuance.
Missing accent in âdemasâ (â âdemÃ¡sâ) and unclear reference âel es un niÃ±oâ. Clarify pronoun antecedents.
Shift between present and past tenses (ânos enseÃ±aâ vs. âhizoâ) creates confusion. Maintain consistent tense to improve coherence.
Overly long sentence in Text 5 lacks punctuation, making it hard to follow. Break into shorter sentences and use commas for clarity.
âreceividoâ should be ârecibidoâ. This anglicized spelling detracts from accuracy. Verify verb forms against a Spanish dictionary.
Subject-verb disagreement in âestÃ¡n atrapadoâ should be âestÃ¡n atrapadosâ. Review agreement in participial phrases.
Sentence describing scene context spans multiple ideas and is hard to parse. Break into two or three sentences to enhance readability.
Conjugation error: âconfesanâ should be âconfiesanâ. Accurate verb forms are essential for credibility.
Insightful comparison of pan-bombing scene reflects effective use of irony. This perceptive link enhances thematic depth.
Strong context-to-epilogue linkage shows consistent thematic focus on loss of innocence. Well done anchoring analysis in plot progression.
Grammatical slip âle va ha darâ should read âle va a darâ. Correct preposition-infinitive structure to maintain accuracy.
Personal reflection in the final paragraph shifts away from textual analysis. Recommend focusing observations on the extract rather than emotional reaction.
Sentence is overly complex and lacks line references. Introduce precise line numbers when linking extract details to support your claims.
Effective comparative link to the lentejas scene shows good thematic coherence. This sustained relevance strengthens interpretive quality.
Misnaming âDoÃ±a MarÃ­a Luisasâ should be âDoÃ±a MarÃ­a Luisaâ. Such errors interrupt reader engagementâverify character names.
